sections.heading: Concept
sections.content:
There are twenty –four consonants in speech in English. Three factors are usually considered in the classification of consonants. These are ‘place of articulation’, ‘manner of articulation and ‘state of the glottis'.

 This chart below shows the classification according to these features.



Manner of articulation	State of the Glottis	Place of Articulation


 Voiceless	      Voiced
		
	p		b		Bilabial	

STOP			t		d		Alveolar

(PLOSIVE)		k		g		Velar

FRICATIVE		f		v		Labio-dental		 
					
θ				Dental
					
s		z		Alveolar
					
S			Palato-Alveolar
					h				Glottal

AFFRICATE		ts		d		Plato-Alveolar

LATERAL					l		Alveolar

LIQUID					r		Alveolar

SEMI-VOWEL				w		Bilabial
							
j		Palatal
							
m		Bilabial

NASAL					h		Alveolar
							
ŋ		Velar

sections.heading: Examples and Practice
sections.content:
Examples of words where the consonants occurs.

1.	/ p /		-	pan, nap

2.	/ b /		-	bud, rub

3.	/ t /		-	tea, seat

4.	/ d / 		-	dip, hide

5.	/k/		-	come, pack

6.	/ g /		-	get, rag

7.	/ f /		-	fork, cough

8.	/ v /		-	van, live

9.	/ θ  /		-	think, both

10.	/   /		-	this, breathe

11.	/ s /		-	sip, place

12.	/ z /		-	zink, has

13.	/s/		-	ship, fish

14.	/З/		-	genre, garage

15.	/ h/		-	hat, behave

16.	/ ts/		-	cheap, teach

17.	/ d З /		-	joy, page

18.	/ l /		-	lock, sell

19.	/ r/		-	rag, borrow

20.	/ w/		-	win, rewind

21.	/ j/		-	yes, new

22.	/m/		-	man, him

23.	/ n /		-	net, rain

24.	/ ŋ/		-	singer, bang
